items.summary: Audit and rewrite the backend authorization logic in backend/main.mo for the updateSiteSettings (and any other admin-protected) actor methods. The root cause is that the admin principal list is either empty at runtime, incorrectly seeded, or the caller identity is being compared against a stale/wrong value. The fix must: (1) capture the deployer's principal at canister install time (using the install message caller) and store it as the initial admin in stable state; (2) compare the runtime caller principal against this stored admin list using Principal.equal; (3) ensure the check is performed correctly so that an authenticated Internet Identity principal that was the deployer is always granted access without throwing an Unauthorized error.

On the frontend, update the useActor hook usage in SiteSettingsSection and the updateSiteSettings mutation in useQueries.ts to ensure the authenticated actor (not the anonymous actor) is always used when calling updateSiteSettings. If the actor is not yet authenticated, the mutation should reject early and surface a clear 'Please log in to save settings' error toast rather than sending an anonymous call that the backend rejects.
